content.problemStatement:
Chef is interested in the history of SnackDown contests. He needs a program to verify if SnackDown was hosted in a given year.

SnackDown was hosted by CodeChef in the following years: 2010, 2015, 2016, 2017 and 2019.


Input Format
The first line contain the number of test-cases TTT.


The first line of each test-case contains a single integer NNN.



Output Format
For each test case print a single line containing the string `"HOSTED"` if SnackDown was hosted in year NNN or `"NOT HOSTED"` otherwise (without quotes).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N representing a year.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing "HOSTED" if SnackDown was hosted in year N, otherwise output "NOT HOSTED".

sampleTestCases.explanation: 2019 is in the hosted list, so output HOSTED. 2018 is not, so output NOT HOSTED.
2010 and 2015 are hosted years, 2020 is not.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
HOSTED
NOT HOSTED
NOT HOSTED
NOT HOSTED
NOT HOSTED

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
NOT HOSTED
NOT HOSTED
NOT HOSTED

solution.approach: Store the five years in which SnackDown was hosted in a constant set or array: {2010, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2019}. For each test case read N and check whether N belongs to this set. Print "HOSTED" if it does, otherwise print "NOT HOSTED". The check can be done with a few equality comparisons or by iterating over the small array.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in O(1) time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables and the constant list of years are stored.
